purpose: Agent-legible execution workboard for reaching an honest ASVS Level 2 posture through file-owned implementation slices.

dispatch_policy: Prefer slices with status ready over blocked or proposed slices.
Never dispatch two slices that both own the same hotspot file.
Treat worker/index.ts and docs/requirements/12-m1-security-decision-record.md as serialized hotspots unless a slice is explicitly extraction-first.
Do not assign docs/security/asvs/** to implementation agents.
After each slice completes, update the workboard before spawning dependent slices.

completed_slices.goal: Harden anti-caching for admin and token-bound sensitive responses.
Extract reusable response security and header policy helpers from the worker router.
residual_risks: Header policy behavior is extracted but not hardened yet; deployment/browser policy tightening remains in A2-header-and-transport-policy.

completed_slices.goal: Extract submit-route request guards so validation and upload policy can evolve without reopening the whole worker router.
residual_risks: Submit request behavior is extracted but policy tightening still depends on B2-method-and-upload-policy.

completed_slices.goal: Implement a coherent browser and transport header policy and document deployment-only controls honestly.
residual_risks: HSTS, HTTPS redirect, and broader TLS posture are still deployment-bound Cloudflare edge controls; this slice documents them honestly but cannot enforce them from local Worker code.
The app-shell CSP is intentionally same-origin-only; any future external origin usage will require an explicit policy update rather than silent drift.

completed_slices.goal: Create one authoritative docs/evidence lane for logging, risky components, and operational security posture.
residual_risks: Operational posture is now centralized in docs, but deployment-side logging, retention, and alert routing still depend on Cloudflare configuration outside this repository.
The evidence lane will drift if future slices change security-relevant behavior without updating the dedicated docs in the same change.

completed_slices.goal: Add explicit method policy and deeper upload validation without widening ownership.
residual_risks: Upload hardening now validates declared MIME against file signatures, but full image decoding and pixel-shape analysis are still outside the current Worker-only implementation.
Method policy is explicit for known routes, but future endpoints must update the route allowlist in the same change or they will drift back toward implicit behavior.

completed_slices.goal: Define and implement storage-side retention and image-metadata handling with clear tests and docs.
residual_risks: Retention boundaries are now explicit in code and R2 object metadata, but automatic deletion still depends on a future operational cleanup path using the recorded deadlines.
Image metadata rejection covers common JPEG and PNG metadata markers; if the accepted file surface expands, the policy helper must expand with it.

completed_slices.goal: Align browser-visible behavior with the implemented edge and upload policy.
tests_run: npm run test:e2e -- --grep "E2E-ADMIN-006 paginates admin table with 20 rows per page"
residual_risks: Full e2e run remains occasionally data-volume flaky in E2E-ADMIN-006 due shared local state assumptions; isolated rerun passed.
Frontend now surfaces METHOD_NOT_ALLOWED clearly, but route-level method mismatches are still expected to be rare under normal UI flows.

slices.goal: Extract reusable response security and header policy helpers from the worker router.
definition_of_done: worker/index.ts delegates response security and header defaults to helper functions or a helper module.
The helper surface is narrow enough that later header-policy slices can edit helpers without broad router conflicts.
Integration coverage still passes after extraction.
slices.agent_prompt: Claim slice X1-edge-response-helpers. You own worker/security.ts and new worker/http-response-policy.ts. Supporting files are worker/index.ts and tests/integration/m1.integration.test.ts. Do not edit docs/security/asvs/** or docs/requirements/12-m1-security-decision-record.md. Extract reusable response security/header helpers out of worker/index.ts with minimal behavior change, keep tests green, and report changed files, tests run, docs updated, controls ready for re-audit, and residual risks.

slices.goal: Extract submit-route request guards so validation and upload policy can evolve without reopening the whole worker router.
definition_of_done: Multipart/image/form validation guards live outside the main route body.
The route still behaves the same before policy hardening starts.
Later upload-policy work can land mostly in worker/request-guards.ts and shared validation files.
slices.agent_prompt: Claim slice X2-submit-request-guards. You own new worker/request-guards.ts plus src/shared/validation.ts and src/shared/domain.ts. Supporting files are worker/index.ts and tests/integration/m1.integration.test.ts. Do not edit docs/security/asvs/**, worker/submission-security.ts, or docs/requirements/12-m1-security-decision-record.md. Extract submit-route request guards with minimal behavior change and report changed files, tests run, docs updated, controls ready for re-audit, and residual risks.

slices.goal: Implement a coherent browser and transport header policy and document deployment-only controls honestly.
definition_of_done: The repo has one coherent response-header policy rather than scattered per-route behavior.
Deployment-only transport controls are documented instead of implied.
Integration tests cover changed header behavior on sensitive and HTML-rendering routes.
slices.agent_prompt: Claim slice A2-header-and-transport-policy after X1-edge-response-helpers is complete. You own worker/security.ts, worker/http-response-policy.ts, and wrangler.jsonc. Supporting files are worker/index.ts, tests/integration/m1.integration.test.ts, and docs/requirements/08-local-testing-and-first-release-runbook.md. Do not edit docs/security/asvs/** or docs/requirements/12-m1-security-decision-record.md. Implement a coherent header policy, document deployment-only transport controls honestly, keep changes minimal, and return the standard handoff.

slices.goal: Add explicit method policy and deeper upload validation without widening ownership.
definition_of_done: Known routes have explicit method behavior instead of implicit 404 fallthrough for supported paths.
Upload rules go beyond MIME and raw byte size where feasible on Workers.
Contract docs and tests reflect the stricter request policy.
slices.agent_prompt: Claim slice B2-method-and-upload-policy after X2-submit-request-guards is complete. You own src/shared/validation.ts, src/shared/domain.ts, and worker/request-guards.ts. Supporting files are worker/index.ts, tests/integration/m1.integration.test.ts, tests/e2e/m1.spec.ts, and docs/requirements/09-m1-api-contract.md. Do not edit docs/security/asvs/**, worker/submission-security.ts, or docs/requirements/12-m1-security-decision-record.md. Implement explicit method policy and deeper upload hardening, update contract docs and tests, and return the standard handoff.

slices.goal: Define and implement storage-side retention and image-metadata handling with clear tests and docs.
definition_of_done: Retention behavior or retention boundaries are represented in code or fail-closed policy helpers rather than vague notes.
Image metadata handling is explicit: stripped, rejected, or documented with evidence if stripping is infeasible.
Storage-side tests cover the new behavior.
slices.agent_prompt: Claim slice C2-retention-and-image-metadata. You own worker/repository.ts, worker/submission-security.ts, and new worker/data-retention.ts. Supporting files are worker/index.ts, tests/security.unit.test.ts, tests/integration/m1.integration.test.ts, and docs/security/crypto-policy.md. Do not edit docs/security/asvs/**, src/features/**, or docs/requirements/12-m1-security-decision-record.md. Implement storage-side retention and image metadata handling with tests and minimal supporting router changes, then return the standard handoff.

slices.goal: Create one authoritative docs/evidence lane for logging, risky components, and operational security posture.
definition_of_done: The repo has one clear place for logging/error-handling expectations.
Risky components and dangerous functionality are documented without touching ASVS artifacts.
Shared ADR and runbook guidance stop drifting across slices.
slices.agent_prompt: Claim slice D2-logging-and-dependency-posture. You own docs/requirements/08-local-testing-and-first-release-runbook.md, docs/requirements/09-m1-api-contract.md, docs/requirements/12-m1-security-decision-record.md, and new docs/security/dependency-risk-register.md plus docs/security/logging-and-error-handling.md. Supporting files are tests/security.unit.test.ts, tests/integration/m1.integration.test.ts, and tests/e2e/m1.spec.ts. Do not edit docs/security/asvs/** or worker/index.ts. Produce the missing docs/evidence layer and return the standard handoff.

slices.goal: Align browser-visible behavior with the implemented edge and upload policy.
definition_of_done: Browser-visible behavior reflects the actual edge and API restrictions.
Client storage or asset assumptions do not undermine the server policy.
User-visible changes are covered by e2e tests.
slices.agent_prompt: Claim slice F1-frontend-security-alignment only after A2-header-and-transport-policy and B2-method-and-upload-policy are complete. You own src/App.tsx, src/features/**, and src/styles.css. Supporting files are tests/e2e/m1.spec.ts and docs/requirements/09-m1-api-contract.md. Do not edit docs/security/asvs/**, worker/submission-security.ts, or docs/requirements/12-m1-security-decision-record.md. Align browser-visible behavior with the implemented server policy and return the standard handoff.

slices.goal: Consolidate tests and requirement docs into an auditor-ready evidence pack before re-audit.
definition_of_done: Behavior changes from earlier slices are reflected in tests and docs with no major evidence gaps.
The repo is ready for another ASVS review without the auditor needing oral history to interpret changes.
ASVS artifacts remain untouched by implementation work.
slices.agent_prompt: Claim slice G1-evidence-consolidation only after all implementation slices are complete. You own tests/security.unit.test.ts, tests/integration/m1.integration.test.ts, tests/e2e/m1.spec.ts, docs/requirements/08-local-testing-and-first-release-runbook.md, docs/requirements/09-m1-api-contract.md, and docs/requirements/12-m1-security-decision-record.md. Supporting files are docs/security/dependency-risk-register.md, docs/security/logging-and-error-handling.md, and docs/security/crypto-policy.md. Do not edit docs/security/asvs/**. Consolidate evidence for re-audit and return the standard handoff.
